channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The slim phone era is (finally) back
item.description: Smartphones have been around long enough that, to the casual observer, their designs seem to have hit a plateau. And on a functional level, that’s more or less true—we’re all essentially holding the same six-inch-ish rectangle, aside from the occasional foldable exception.
item.dc:creator: Sam Byford, Fast Company

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance as a team to make the case for their cost-cutting efforts amid growing pushback.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 Common Mistakes in Identity-Driven Customer Data Strategies
item.description: Learn how business leaders can improve their data strategy, manage vendors effectively, and accelerate digital transformation by overcoming common mistakes in identity-driven customer data strategies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Securing Your Digital Identity in the Era of GenAI
item.description: Learn about the growing threat of compromised passwords and account IDs empowered by generative AI, and take steps to protect yourself with Apple's Passwords app.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nintendo reveals the new Virtual Game Card feature, an easier way to manage your digital Switch games
item.description: Nintendo has announced a new Switch feature called the Virtual Game Card, making it easier to lend out digital games.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: ClickFix malware tricks you into infecting your own Windows PC
item.description: Hackers are tricking people into installing password-stealing malware by making them press keyboard shortcuts under the pretense of proving they're not bots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: I'm not waiting for a dedicated Xbox handheld any longer thanks to the new Backbone One: Xbox Edition
item.description: The Backbone One: Xbox Edition, an Xbox version of the excellent mobile controller, has been revealed.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia GTC 2025: News and insights
item.description: Nvidia GTC 2025 has become a landmark event, solidifying the company’s position at the forefront of the AI technology. This year the major theme was agentic AI, which represents a shift from data generation to AI systems capable of autonomous reasoning and decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Using a smartwatch could be a game-changer for people with diabetes, new research suggests
item.description: New research indicates that wearing a smartwatch can help Type 2 Diabetes patients stick to an exercise routine and manage their condition.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Connecting with Gen Z: A guide to boosting employee engagement
item.description: When I asked ChatGPT what is important to Generation Z in preparation for this story, this (abbreviated) answer came up: “Generation Z wants a balance between work and personal life and has high expectations of employers in terms of career and security. This also includes the need for feedback.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cleveland Clinic and G42 join forces to drive AI-Powered healthcare innovation
item.description: Cleveland Clinic and G42 have entered into a strategic collaboration to advance the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) in healthcare, marking yet another major step in realizing AI’s potential in medicine.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A Venus flytrap wasp? Scientists uncover an ancient insect preserved in amber that snatched its prey
item.description: Scientists have discovered an ancient wasp with a body like a Venus flytrap to seize and snatch its prey.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Airman charged with posing as teen on Roblox to coerce 9-year-old into sharing explicit images
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A U.S. airman has been charged with coercing a 9-year-old girl to share sexually explicit images of herself, after he posed as a 13-year-old on the gaming site Roblox.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada bars Tesla from rebate program over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canada has barred electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla from all of its rebate programs and banned the Texas-based automotive company from future electric vehicle rebate programs over President Trump’s tariff back-and-forth with Ottawa.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: DOGE staffer allegedly ran company providing services to hacking group
item.description: US Department of Government Efficiency tech advisor Edward Coristine previously ran a small infrastructure provider that offered services to a cybercriminal group, it has been alleged.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Graham: 'Very sensitive information' included in group chat
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham has acknowledged that "very sensitive information" was discussed among high-level officials via messaging app Signal, but sees the snafu as a "lessons learned" episode.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: GameStop adopts policy to buy Bitcoin, joining other firms
item.description: GameStop Corp., the struggling video-game retailer that became a favorite of retail traders during the meme stock frenzy in 2021, said its board has approved a plan to add Bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok is facing a rare meme drought. Inside the Great Meme Depression of 2025
item.description: People with a healthy limit on their screen time probably haven’t noticed—but there’s been a meme shortage this March. On TikTok, some have declared a full-blown “Meme Drought,” dubbing it the “Great Meme Depression of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Tiny electric vehicle takes half a parking space
item.description: Toyota's compact electric FT-Me is designed to tackle challenges of city living while offering an accessible and eco-friendly transportation solution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gee whiz, Gboard! An invaluable new Android typing upgrade
item.description: Ahh — don’t you just love the feeling of a fresh ‘n’ zesty virtual upgrade? Here in the land o’ Android, that doesn’t mean only major new Android versions (and, alas, the woeful waiting that often accompanies ’em).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Researchers in limbo as Columbia bows to Trump’s demands in bid to restore $400M federal funding
item.description: Researchers at Columbia University remain in limbo after the Ivy League institution bowed to the Trump administration’s demands for unprecedented changes in a bid to restore some $400 million in federal funds cut.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2
